title: argemone brevicornuta
intro: Argemone brevicornuta, also known as the short-spurred prickly poppy, is a plant species belonging to the poppy family (Papaveraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The plant's seeds contain sanguinarine, a compound that has been found to have antimicrobial and antiparasitic properties, offering potential health benefits in treating conditions like diarrhea, fever, and skin infections. In traditional medicine, the plant's seeds and roots are often prepared into herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties.
